body: The email dropdown trigger has `aria-haspopup="menu"` but `aria-expanded` is hard-coded to "false" and never updated when the menu opens/closes. This also doesn't provide keyboard behavior expected for a menu button (open on Enter/Space, close on Escape, and close on outside click/focus move). Update the JS/markup to keep `aria-expanded` in sync and ensure the menu is usable via keyboard (and consider adding appropriate roles/aria-controls).

`delay-500` is used for the last skill card, but `src/styles/brutalist.css` only defines delay classes up to `delay-400`, so this delay won't take effect. Either add a `.animate-on-scroll.delay-500` rule in the CSS or adjust the component to only use supported delay classes.

"System for registering and tracking financial credits." + : locale === "en" && project.title === "Formless" + ? "Editable and customizable form with tables and bookmarks, exportable to docx." + : project.description}
body: Project descriptions are being conditionally translated by matching on `project.title` string literals. This is brittle (title changes will silently break translations) and mixes i18n logic into the rendering layer. Prefer moving localized descriptions into the `PROJECTS` data (e.g., keyed by an id/slug) or into the i18n dictionary so rendering can just pick `project.description[locale]` or similar.

This PR description mentions removing React/Radix-related dependencies, but `package.json` still includes React-specific packages (e.g. `lucide-react`, `@radix-ui/react-*`) that appear unused in `src/` and typically require React as a peer. If the goal is fully eliminating React from the project, remove these unused dependencies and regenerate the lockfile to avoid lingering React-related packages/warnings.

body: The inline email dropdown <script> contains TypeScript-only syntax (`as HTMLElement` and `let timeout: number`), which will be a runtime syntax error in the browser unless this script is explicitly compiled as TypeScript. Remove the type assertions/annotations or mark the script to be processed as TS (e.g. `lang="ts"`) so the emitted code is valid JavaScript.

🟡 **Email dropdown `aria-expanded` is never updated by JavaScript** The email dropdown button in `NewPresentation.astro` has `aria-expanded="false"` hardcoded at line 81, but the JavaScript click handler (lines 213-216) only toggles the `hidden` class on the menu — it never updates `aria-expanded` to reflect the actual open/closed state. The previous React implementation used Radix UI's `DropdownMenu` which managed `aria-expanded` automatically. Screen readers will always announce this button as collapsed, even when the dropdown menu is visible. ```suggestion trigger.addEventListener('click', (e) => { e.preventDefault(); menu?.classList.toggle('hidden'); const isExpanded = !menu?.classList.contains('hidden'); trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', String(isExpanded)); }); ``` --- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*

🟡 **Missing `delay-500` in custom scroll animation CSS definitions** `NewSkills.astro:51` applies `delay-500` to the 6th skill item (Python), but `brutalist.css` only defines custom delay classes up to `delay-400` (lines 172-175). While Tailwind's built-in `delay-500` utility provides `transition-delay: 500ms` and will function correctly, the custom CSS pattern wraps all other delay definitions inside `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)` with the compound selector `.animate-on-scroll.delay-*`. The `delay-500` class breaks this pattern by relying solely on Tailwind's utility instead. ```suggestion .animate-on-scroll.delay-400 { transition-delay: 400ms; } .animate-on-scroll.delay-500 { transition-delay: 500ms; } ``` --- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*

The scroll-animation script assumes `IntersectionObserver` exists; in older browsers/environments where it’s missing, this will throw and (combined with the CSS) can leave content hidden. Add a feature check (and/or `try/catch`) and fall back to immediately adding `is-visible` to all `.animate-on-scroll` elements when observers aren’t available.

body: The CSS hover rule forces the email menu to `display: block` on hover even when it still has the `hidden` class, which can desync the visible state from `aria-expanded` (it may remain `false` while the menu is visible). Prefer using a single source of truth: either remove the hover CSS and let JS toggle visibility + `aria-expanded`, or implement the dropdown purely in CSS/HTML (e.g., `<details>`/`<summary>`) and drop `aria-expanded` management.

The dropdown JS registers `document.addEventListener('click', …)` inside the loop over triggers, which adds a new global click handler per trigger and is easy to duplicate if more dropdowns are added. Move the outside-click handler registration outside the loop (or use a single delegated handler) and have it close the currently-open menu(s). Also consider wrapping `navigator.clipboard.writeText` in `try/catch` and handling failure (non-secure context / denied permission) so the UI doesn’t get stuck in a “copied” state incorrectly.

body: `<time datetime={study.year.replace(' - ', '/')}>` produces values like `2019/2024`, which isn’t a valid `datetime` value and may confuse assistive tech/parsers. For year ranges, consider omitting `datetime`, or split into two `<time>` elements (start/end) with valid ISO values (e.g., `datetime="2019"` and `datetime="2024"`).

The `.animate-on-scroll` base style sets `opacity: 0` under `prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference`, so if the JS observer doesn’t run (JS disabled, blocked by CSP, runtime error, or `IntersectionObserver` unsupported), large portions of the page will remain permanently invisible. Consider making content visible by default and only applying the hidden/translated initial state when a `js` (or similar) class is present on `<html>` (added by a tiny early script), or add a `<noscript>`/fallback rule that forces `.animate-on-scroll { opacity: 1; transform: none; }`.

✅ **Resolved**: The previous bug incorrectly claimed aria-expanded is never updated by JavaScript. Looking at the actual code in NewPresentation.astro, the openMenu() function (line 199) calls trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true') and closeMenu() (line 206) calls trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false'). The click handler at lines 232-240 calls these functions. The previous bug was factually wrong.

✅ **Resolved**: Looking at brutalist.css, line 176 defines .animate-on-scroll.delay-500 { transition-delay: 500ms; }. The delay-500 class IS defined in the custom CSS. The previous bug was factually incorrect.

body: 🟡 **Conflicting `block` and `flex` display classes on email dropdown link** The `` element for the "Send email" option in the email dropdown has both `block` and `flex` Tailwind utility classes: `class="block px-4 py-3 text-brutalist-white hover:bg-brutalist-white/10 flex items-center gap-2"`. Both `block` (`display: block`) and `flex` (`display: flex`) set the `display` CSS property. In Tailwind v3, `flex` happens to win because it's generated later in the CSS output, so the element renders as flexbox (which is what `items-center gap-2` require). However, the `block` class is dead/overridden code that indicates conflicting intent and would break the layout if Tailwind's internal generation order ever changed. ```suggestion class="px-4 py-3 text-brutalist-white hover:bg-brutalist-white/10 flex items-center gap-2" ``` --- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*
